label: Minor superficial burn, small area, without high-risk features
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of small superficial minor burn.

No deep/partial/full-thickness burn concern, circumferential burn, face/hand/genital/major joint location, inhalation injury, infection, chemical/electrical burn, high-risk host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed burn plan.
exclusion_criteria: Deep, white, charred, circumferential, large, face/hand/genital/major joint burn, inhalation injury, chemical/electrical burn, infection, fever, or systemic illness.
Diabetes, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, unreliable wound care, or specialist-directed burn plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, burn care, or the ED as instructed, especially if pain, redness, or healing is not improving.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports minor-burn aftercare concepts, cooling/protection, avoiding blister disruption or unsafe substances, and escalation for large, deep, infected, high-risk-location, chemical/electrical, inhalation-associated, or high-risk-host burns. Autonomous Phase 1801-1850 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed small superficial minor burn without high-risk depth/location/mechanism, inhalation injury, infection, high-risk host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed burn plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports minor-burn aftercare concepts, cooling/protection, avoiding blister disruption or unsafe substances, and escalation for large, deep, infected, high-risk-location, chemical/electrical, inhalation-associated, or high-risk-host burns. Autonomous Phase 1801-1850 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed small superficial minor burn without high-risk depth/location/mechanism, inhalation injury, infection, high-risk host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed burn plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of small superficial minor burn
no deep burn, inhalation injury, high-risk location, or burn infection documented
no chemical/electrical burn, high-risk host, pregnancy, pediatric, or specialist-directed burn pathway
